rules.rule: kremis-core must remain PURE (Separation Law): no async, no network, no I/O. Reject any import of tokio, reqwest, std::net, or other async/network crates. Flag any use of: unwrap(), expect(), panic!() (clippy::deny in this project); f32/f64 arithmetic (float_arithmetic is deny); Randomness, timestamps, or non-deterministic logic (Determinism Law); Silent error conversion to None/default without caller visibility (Precision Law). For edge mutations (insert_edge, increment_edge): verify that node-existence checks are consistent across Graph (in-memory) and RedbGraph (persistent) backends. increment_edge must not create edges for non-existent nodes. For property storage (store_property): verify that repeated ingestion of the same signal does not silently accumulate duplicate (attribute, value) pairs.

The MCP bridge must mirror the HTTP API contract exactly. When parsing JSON responses, field names must match the actual HTTP response structs defined in apps/kremis/src/api/types.rs. RetractResponse fields are: success (bool), new_weight (i64|null), error (string|null). The fields retracted, from_node, to_node do NOT exist in the HTTP response. Flag any JSON field lookup that does not correspond to a field in the matching HTTP response type. Missing tests for MCP tool handlers must be flagged.

Verify that json_mode: bool parameters are actually used to produce JSON output. Parameters prefixed with _ (e.g. _json_mode) are silently ignored — flag them. Serialization errors must NOT be silenced with unwrap_or_default().
Multi-value environment variables (e.g. KREMIS_CORS_ORIGINS) must be parsed as comma-separated values, not wrapped as a single-element Vec.

Every feat: commit requires a MINOR version bump; every fix: commit requires a PATCH bump on line 10 (the workspace version field). Verify consistency with: docs/api/overview.mdx, apps/kremis/tests/types_tests.rs, docs/openapi.yml.
CLI flag names must match apps/kremis/src/cli/mod.rs exactly. API docs must be consistent with docs/openapi.yml. Do not reference private directories: .claude/, _archive/, social/.

other.content: Kremis is a deterministic knowledge graph engine written in Rust. The project follows 4 Fundamental Laws: Determinism (no randomness, no timestamps in core), Precision (no silent error swallowing), Security (no unwrap/expect/panic), and Separation (core crate is pure sync with no I/O). The workspace has three crates: kremis-core (pure logic), kremis (HTTP API server), kremis-mcp (MCP bridge to the API).
